title: Expose borrowed iteration with iter
scenario: Callers often need to inspect a collection wrapper without consuming it. A borrowed iterator exposes a view without leaking fields.

hints.body: Focus on the one public API shape requested by the lesson.
Prefer standard traits, typed errors, borrowed views, and explicit boundary conversions.
A canonical idiomatic solution is shown below.

completionExplanation: This lesson keeps wrap-borrowed-iter explicit in the type system and checks public behavior without relying on panics or unchecked shortcuts.
